title: Lusail International Circuit
sub: A fast, flowing desert lap run under floodlights: an almost unbroken chain of medium-to-high-speed sweeps that pile on relentless lateral G. The most physically brutal circuit of the year on the neck and the tyres.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The 1.068km main straight into the Turn 1 right, then the fast, committed Turn 2-4 sweeps that immediately load the tyres.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The relentless middle: a chain of medium-to-high-speed sweeps taken barely off the throttle, the highest sustained lateral loading on the calendar.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: More fast direction changes through Turn 11-14 and the quick final corners that fire cars back onto the long main straight.

tyreAlloc: Hardest in the range · C1 / C2 / C3
stratNote: The near-constant high-speed cornering hammers the tyre sidewalls so hard that Pirelli has mandated a maximum stint length here, forcing at least a two-stop regardless of pace. Managing that cap, not saving tyres, is the game.
spots: End of the 1.068km main straight + DRS. The main braking and overtaking zone.
A dive after the second DRS zone into a slower corner.
Following through the fast middle sector is hard, so most passes happen at the straight-ends.
otNote: One long straight with DRS into Turn 1 gives the clearest chance; the rest of the lap is flat-out sweeps where dirty air makes following tough. Track position and the tyre-cap strategy matter most.
corners: End of the 1.068km main straight + DRS. The prime overtaking spot and heaviest braking zone.
Quick, committed right-left immediately loading the tyres.
High-speed right onto the flowing middle.
Medium-high-speed direction changes barely off throttle.
Fast right at the far point of the lap.
More committed high-speed changes of direction.
Quick flick loading the tyres before the run home.
The quick final corners launching cars back onto the main straight and DRS.
geo: Built in 2004 for MotoGP and an F1 venue since 2021, Lusail races at night under lights, ringed by artificial grass that keeps the desert sand off the racing line.
An almost unbroken sequence of fast sweeps makes this the highest sustained lateral-load circuit of the year, punishing on the neck and merciless on tyre sidewalls.
The lateral loads are so severe that Pirelli imposes a maximum stint length, forcing a mandated two-stop and turning the race into a flat-out sprint between stops.
Even at night the Gulf heat and humidity are intense, adding to the physical toll of the most demanding lap on the calendar.
